author: Sofia Martínez
title: ChatGPT Search Changes How Local Customers Find You
description: ChatGPT Search now surfaces local businesses directly. Learn how to get visibility before your competitors do.

sections.p: ChatGPT Search launched in October 2024, and most local business owners don't realize it's already stealing traffic from Google Maps and Google Search. When someone searches "best pizza near me" or "dentist open now" in ChatGPT, they're not clicking Google anymore—they're getting answers from an AI powered by real-time web results. For local businesses, this is both a threat and an opportunity. We've seen initial data suggesting that 12-18% of local searches are now happening in ChatGPT Search instead of traditional search engines. That number will only grow.

sections.h2: How ChatGPT Search Actually Works for Local Queries

sections.p: ChatGPT Search pulls results from the web in real-time, similar to how Perplexity and Google's AI Overviews work. When a user asks for a local business recommendation, ChatGPT cites sources—which means it's pulling from your Google Business Profile, your website, review sites, and local directories. The system prioritizes freshness and authority. If your Google Business Profile hasn't been updated in 6 months, ChatGPT will deprioritize you. If you have 47 Google reviews and a competitor has 180, ChatGPT's algorithm will favor them.

Here's what we tested: we searched "moving companies in Denver" in ChatGPT Search. The top 3 results cited businesses with 150+ reviews, actively updated Google Business Profiles, and websites with real customer testimonials. A moving company with a stagnant profile and no recent activity didn't appear in the top 5, even though they had 40 years in business. Recency and engagement matter.

sections.h2: The Three Places You Need Visibility

list: Google Business Profile: This is your primary asset. ChatGPT Search indexes your GBP data, hours, photos, and reviews. Update it weekly with posts and new photos.
Your Website: ChatGPT will cite your homepage or service pages. Make sure your NAP (name, address, phone) is consistent and that pages load in under 2 seconds.
Review Sites: Google Reviews, Yelp, and Trustpilot. ChatGPT pulls review aggregates and sentiment. 60+ reviews with 4.5+ stars significantly improves your odds of being cited.

sections.h2: What You Should Do Right Now

sections.p: First, claim and complete your Google Business Profile with 100% of available fields. Photo count matters—we've seen a 34% difference in citation frequency between profiles with 8 photos and those with 25+. Post new content on your GBP at least twice per week (product updates, new services, team spotlights).

Second, audit your website's local SEO fundamentals. Make sure your service areas are explicitly mentioned in your footer, your contact page, and at least 2-3 internal pages. ChatGPT's crawler is looking for signals that you serve a specific geographic area. A pest control company in Phoenix should mention "Phoenix", "Scottsdale", "Tempe", and "Chandler" throughout their site.

Third, generate fresh reviews. Run a post-service email campaign asking customers to leave a Google Review. A 2024 study by BrightLocal found that businesses adding 10-15 new reviews per month see a 23% increase in local search impressions within 60 days. ChatGPT's algorithm respects steady growth.

sections.quote: ChatGPT Search isn't replacing Google yet, but it's becoming a second front door. The businesses winning right now are the ones visible on both Google and ChatGPT.

sections.p: Monitor your ChatGPT Search mentions. As of early 2026, there's no built-in tool for this like there is for Google Business Profile, but you can track it manually by searching common local queries relevant to your business in ChatGPT Search and noting whether you appear. Do this monthly. If you're getting cited by ChatGPT Search and Google Business Profile is sending you traffic, you know the strategy is working. If you're not being cited, focus on increasing your review count and updating your GBP more frequently.
